title: potentilla polyphylla var. kinabaluensis
intro: Potentilla polyphylla var. kinabaluensis, also known as the Mount Kinabalu cinquefoil, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's roots and leaves contain flavonoids and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Potentilla polyphylla var. kinabaluensis, including tea and infusions, are used to support overall health and well-being, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
